admin.title: Command stations
admin.subtitle: DCC command-station catalogue. Attach stations to layouts from the layout edit dialog.

delete.title: Delete command station "{{name}}"
delete.message: This cannot be undone. The station will be detached from every layout it was attached to.

fields.name: Name
fields.speedStepsHelp: DCC speed resolution forwarded to dcc-bus. Default 128.
fields.pollIntervalMsHelp: State-feed polling cadence for drivers without push. 0 uses the daemon default (750 ms).
fields.heartbeatSecsHelp: WebSocket ping interval. Default 2 s. If the ping does not arrive before the dead-man, the vehicle will be emergency-stopped.
fields.deadmanSecsHelp: Idle window after which the daemon applies emergency stop. Must be longer than the heartbeat. Default 6 s.
fields.z21ServerEnabledHelp: Independent of the outbound connection type. Z21 apps connect to the dcc-bus host on UDP port 21105. Pairing uses CV3 and CV4 or the numeric keypad.
fields.z21IpStickiness: Session bound to IP (IP stickiness — read the note below)
fields.z21IpStickinessHelp: When enabled, the handset session is bound to the client IP, not the UDP port — more convenient for users. After a reconnect with a new port (common with DHCP) pairing stays active. Important! Your router must use IP stickiness or assign fixed IPs to handsets manually, otherwise another handset may receive an IP previously used by another user's handset and could control their vehicles!
fields.z21ServerDisableWarn: Disabling the server stops new pairings. Active handset sessions stop working after the dcc-bus daemon restarts.
fields.withrottleServerEnabledHelp: WiFred handsets connect to the dcc-bus host on TCP port 12090. Pairing uses a 6-digit code via the pairing virtual locomotive or the device name.
fields.withrottleServerDisableWarn: Disabling the server stops new pairings. Active WiFred sessions stop working after the dcc-bus daemon restarts.
fields.maxLoconetSlotsHelp: How many slots BigFred may lease on this command station; the rest stay free for physical throttles. Default 80.
fields.allocatePhysicalSlotsHelp: When on (default), BigFred claims exclusive IN_USE like a physical FRED — it cannot take a loco another throttle already drives, and a FRED cannot take one BigFred holds. When off, BigFred may share a slot already held by a FRED (legacy piggyback). Changing this restarts dcc-bus.
fields.idleTimeoutSecsHelp: After this idle period a remote handset (WlanMaus/Z21/WiFred) loses its slot. 0 disables idle release. Default 60 s.
fields.bootStopEnabledHelp: When enabled, dcc-bus sends emergency stop to every vehicle in the system once after start.
fields.singleVehicleControl: Only one vehicle can be controlled at a time
fields.singleVehicleControlHelp: When enabled, selecting or driving a different vehicle or consist stops the user's other moving vehicles (speed > 1). Lent-out vehicles are not stopped.

slotsDiag.title: DCC-Bus slot diagnostics
slotsDiag.subtitle: Live LocoNet slot leases, per-user caps, and FRED headroom. Read-only.
slotsDiag.unlimitedSlots: {{used}} active leases (no LocoNet budget on this station)
slotsDiag.releaseConfirm: Release the slot for address {{addr}}? The locomotive will be emergency-stopped.
